Definition/Description: The subject is barely visible; emphasizes the vast landscape or location.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Establish the vastness, show isolation, or emphasize scale.
Prompt Example: Extreme wide shot of [img 1] standing alone in a vast, empty frozen tundra. The subject is small in the distance, surrounded by massive snowy mountains. Cinematic lighting

Definition/Description: Shows the full subject (head to toe) and the environment.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Establish context, show subject's relationship to surroundings.
Prompt Example: Wide shot of [img 1] standing full body in a snowstorm. She is wearing fur armor, boots visible, centered in a snowy forest clearing. Environmental context.

Definition/Description: Frames the subject from head to toe, filling the frame roughly top to bottom.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Show full body language and costume/attire clearly.
Prompt Example: Full body shot of [img 1] from head to toe. She stands confidently in the snow, holding a weapon at her side. Detailed costume design

Definition/Description: Knees up. Also known as a "3/4 shot."
Primary Purpose/Effect: More focus on the subject while retaining some environment context.
Prompt Example: Medium long shot of [img 1] framed from the knees up. She is walking through deep snow, looking towards the horizon. 3/4 angle view.

Definition/Description: Mid-thighs up. Originates from Westerns (to show the gun holster).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Powerful and confident framing, emphasizes action readiness.
Prompt Example: Cowboy shot of [img 1] framed from mid-thigh up. Focus on her hands resting on her belt, confident stance, snowy background with soft bokeh.

Definition/Description: Waist up. The standard storytelling shot.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Captures body language and face, ideal for dialogue.
Prompt Example: Medium shot of [img 1] from the waist up. She is looking directly at the camera with a serious expression. Snowflakes falling in the foreground.

Definition/Description: Chest/Shoulders up.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Focuses on the face but keeps some distance for context.
Prompt Example: Medium close-up of [img 1] framed from chest up. Focus on her face and fur collar. The wind is blowing her braided hair. Intimate and dramatic

Definition/Description: Head and neck only.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Pure emotion and facial expression, high intimacy.
Prompt Example: Close-up of [img 1], framing only her head and neck. Her piercing eyes looking into the lens. Frosty breath visible in the cold air.

Definition/Description: Focuses on a specific detail (just the eyes, a mouth, a finger pressing a button).
Primary Purpose/Effect: High intensity, highlights critical detail, pure focus.
Prompt Example: Extreme close-up on the eyes of [img 1]. Macro detail of the iris, snowflakes caught in her eyelashes, war paint texture. Intense gaze

Definition/Description: Microscopic level detail (e.g., texture of fabric, a bug on a leaf).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Reveal texture or highly detailed, unseen elements.
Prompt Example: Macro shot of the texture of [img 1]'s fur armor and the frost on her skin. Microscopic details of snow crystals and leather grain

Definition/Description: Neutral perspective.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Realism; how we see the world naturally, creates connection.
Prompt Example: Eye-level shot of [img 1] facing the camera. Neutral perspective, realistic documentary style portrait in the snow.

Definition/Description: Camera looks **up** at the subject.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Makes them look powerful, dominant, or scary.
Prompt Example: Low angle shot looking up at [img 1]. She towers over the camera, looking powerful and dominant against a stormy sky.

Definition/Description: Camera looks **down** at the subject.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Makes them look small, weak, or vulnerable.
Prompt Example: High angle shot looking down at [img 1]. She is looking up towards the camera, snow falling onto her face. Vulnerable perspective.

Definition/Description: Looking straight down (90 degrees) from the sky.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Graphic and map-like, often used for scale or tracking.
Prompt Example: Direct overhead bird's eye view of [img 1] standing in the snow. Top-down angle showing her shadow stretching on the white ground.

Definition/Description: Extremely low, looking up from the ground level.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Exaggerates height/size, gives an unusual perspective.
Prompt Example: Worm's eye view from the ground looking up at [img 1]'s boots and legs, with her face distant in the sky. Ant-man perspective

Definition/Description: The camera is tilted sideways (tilted horizon).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Creates unease, disorientation, or chaos.
Prompt Example: Dutch angle shot of [img 1], tilted horizon line. Disorienting and chaotic atmosphere, battle ready pose, dynamic composition.

Definition/Description: Looking past one person’s shoulder at another.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Essential for dialogue, grounds the conversation.
Prompt Example: Over-the-shoulder shot looking past a blurry Viking warrior in the foreground, focusing on [img 1] who is staring back at them.

Definition/Description: The camera *is* the character’s eyes.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Viewer experiences what the character sees, high immersion.
Prompt Example: First person POV shot of someone approaching [img 1]. She is looking directly at the viewer, hand extended as if greeting the camera.

Definition/Description: High angle, arm’s length, facing the subject.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Common in social media aesthetics, informal, close.
Prompt Example: Handheld selfie angle of [img 1] looking into the lens, arm extended. Casual vlog style, snowy mountains in the immediate background.

Definition/Description: Camera sits directly on the floor/surface, often with shallow depth of field.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Immersive close-up of the ground or environment.
Prompt Example: Ground level shot, camera resting on the snow. Shallow depth of field with snow crystals in foreground, [img 1] walking in the background.

Definition/Description: No movement. The camera is locked off on a tripod.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Focus on subtle subject animation or internal conflict.
Prompt Example: Static camera shot of [img 1]. She stands perfectly still while snow falls heavily around her and the wind moves her hair. Tripod shot.

Definition/Description: The camera rotates horizontally on a fixed axis (like turning your head).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Follow a subject, reveal a new part of the scene.
AI Prompt Keyword: Pan Left, Pan Right, Horizontal Move
Prompt Example: Camera pans slowly from left to right, starting on a snowy tree and revealing [img 1] standing in the distance.

Definition/Description: The camera rotates vertically on a fixed axis (like nodding your head).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Reveal a subject's height or a dramatic vertical structure.
AI Prompt Keyword: Tilt Up, Tilt Down, Vertical Move
Prompt Example: Camera tilts up, starting at [img 1]'s boots in the snow and moving up to reveal her face and the mountain peak behind her.

Definition/Description: The lens focal length changes to get closer.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Focus attention, flatten the background.
Prompt Example: Slow zoom in on [img 1]'s face. The background compresses as the camera gets tighter on her expression.

Definition/Description: The lens widens to reveal more context.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Reveal context, shift focus to the environment.
Prompt Example: Zoom out from [img 1]'s eye to reveal she is standing on the edge of a massive cliff. Establishing the environment.

Definition/Description: The *entire* camera physically moves up or down (elevator motion).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Change the subject's relationship to the ground or surrounding objects.
Prompt Example: Camera pedestals up, rising from the snow level to eye-level with [img 1], revealing her stature against the mountains.

Definition/Description: The camera physically moves *forward* toward the subject.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Increases intensity/intimacy, draws attention to a realization.
Prompt Example: Smooth cinematic dolly in towards [img 1]. The camera physically pushes forward through the snow, getting closer to her.

Definition/Description: The camera physically moves *backward*.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Signals "leaving" or "conclusion," reveals the scale of the environment.
Prompt Example: Cinematic dolly out from [img 1]. The camera physically moves backward, starting close to her face and retreating to show her alone.

Definition/Description: The camera physically moves sideways (parallel to the subject).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Keeps pace with a moving subject, shows a dynamic journey.
AI Prompt Keyword: Truck Left, Track Right, Parallel Motion
Prompt Example: Truck right camera movement. The camera slides sideways parallel to [img 1] as she stands in profile, revealing the army behind her

Definition/Description: The camera circles around the subject (360 degrees or partial).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Heroic and cinematic, shows the subject in their environment dynamically.
Prompt Example: Camera orbits 360 degrees around [img 1]. She stands still in the center while the snowy background spins slowly behind her.

Definition/Description: The camera sweeps up and over the scene from a high mechanical arm.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Grand reveals, establishing large environments from above.
Prompt Example: Crane shot starting low on [img 1] and sweeping high up into the air, looking down at the snowy valley below

Definition/Description: The camera moves backward while zooming in (or vice versa).
Primary Purpose/Effect: The background warps while the subject stays the same size, creating disorientation.
Prompt Example: Dolly zoom effect on [img 1]. The background mountains appear to warp and grow larger while she stays the same size. Vertigo effect

Definition/Description: An incredibly fast, sudden zoom in.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Comedic or dramatic impact, shock effect.
Prompt Example: Sudden crash zoom into [img 1]'s face. The camera snaps from a wide shot to an extreme close-up instantly. Dramatic impact.

Definition/Description: Fast, flying movement that banks, dives, and weaves through a space.
Primary Purpose/Effect: High energy, dynamic, immersive sense of speed.
Prompt Example: Fast FPV drone shot flying through a snowy canyon and swooping past [img 1]. High speed, dynamic motion blur.

Definition/Description: Time freezes (or slows down) while the camera moves around the subject (Matrix style).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Highlights a critical, slowed moment in time, dynamic perspective.
Prompt Example: Bullet time effect. Time is frozen with snowflakes suspended in mid-air, while the camera smoothly rotates 180 degrees around [img 1]

Definition/Description: Adds artificial "shake" to mimic a human operator.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Gritty and realistic, adds immediacy and urgency.
Prompt Example: Handheld camera movement following [img 1]. The image has slight shake and organic motion, documentary war reporter style.

Definition/Description: The camera rotates its horizon line (spinning the world upside down).
Primary Purpose/Effect: Creates extreme disorientation or visual flair.
Prompt Example: Camera roll. The horizon line spins 360 degrees while focused on [img 1], turning the snowy world upside down and back again

Definition/Description: The focus shifts from a foreground object to a background object (or vice versa) without the camera moving.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Directs the viewer's attention, reveals a new object/person.
Prompt Example: Rack focus shot. Starts focused on a sword in the foreground, then shifts focus to [img 1]'s face in the background.

Definition/Description: Starts close on a detail and pulls back to show a surprising environment.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Dramatic reveal of context or scale.
Prompt Example: Pull back reveal. Camera starts on the detail of [img 1]'s eye, then pulls back rapidly to show she is standing on top of a burning village

Definition/Description: The camera moves *through* a window, keyhole, or object to enter a new space.
Primary Purpose/Effect: Seamless transition between spaces, dynamic and immersive.
Prompt Example: Camera flies through a gap in the trees and past falling snow to land on a medium shot of [img 1].
